name: I.B.E.W. Lu 66
description_short: IBEW Local 66 Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union in Pasadena, TX offering checking, savings, lending, and member financial services.
description_long:
IBEW Local 66 Federal Credit Union operates as a member-owned financial institution serving the electrical workers union community and eligible members in the Pasadena, Texas area. The organization holds routing number 313083837 and maintains a full-service credit union platform accessible through their website mycu66.org and mobile application. Founded on the principle of union solidarity, the credit union emphasizes local presence while modernizing digital banking capabilities. The institution markets itself as "always been local" while expanding digital-first conveniences for members.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of deposit accounts including five checking account tiers (Union e-Express, Union Select, Union Advantage, Union Pride, and others), regular savings, money market accounts, club accounts (Christmas and Union Club), and certificates of deposit. On the lending side, they provide auto financing (advertised at rates as low as 1.95% APR), boat and marine loans, camper/trailer loans, classic car loans, motorcycle loans, personal/signature loans, recreational vehicle loans, share secure loans, and seasonal Christmas loan programs. Digital conveniences include debit cards with access to 30,000+ Co-Op network ATMs at no charge, direct deposit, e-statements, remote deposit, mobile banking, skip-a-payment programs, and a relationship rewards system called Power by You.

The credit union distinguishes itself through union-affiliated membership requirements, competitive auto loan rates prominently featured on their homepage, and free access to an extensive nationwide ATM network via the Co-Op system. Their product naming strategy (Union Pride, Union Advantage, Union e-Express) reinforces member identity. They offer optional Courtesy Pay overdraft coverage (assessed at $25 per transaction when used) and multiple overdraft protection options including transfers from savings or dedicated lines of credit. The mobile-first approach emphasizes convenience and accessibility for working union members.

Honestly assessed, this is a legitimate and well-established credit union appropriate for eligible members seeking competitive rates and union-aligned financial services. Primary limitations include membership eligibility restrictions (IBEW union affiliation or family connection required), paper statement fees ($3.50 per mailed statement unless waived by account type), and Courtesy Pay overdraft fees that could accumulate during financial hardship. The website provides clear product information but lacks published lending qualification criteria, rate disclosures for products beyond auto loans, and specific eligibility requirements for membership.
services: Union Pride Checking Account with rewards and relationship benefits
Union e-Express, Union Select, and Union Advantage checking accounts
Regular Shares savings accounts
Family Savings Plan
Union Advantage and Union Pride Money Market Accounts
Certificates of Deposit with published dividend rates
Auto loans (rates from 1.95% APR for new and used vehicles)
Boat and marine loans
Camper and trailer loans
Motorcycle loans
Personal and signature loans
Recreational vehicle loans
Christmas Club and Union Club accounts
MasterCard debit cards with Co-Op ATM network access
Direct deposit with qualifying income verification
Remote deposit capture via mobile app
E-statements with enhanced security
Skip-A-Payment program
Overdraft Protection (savings transfer, money market transfer, or credit line options)
Courtesy Pay overdraft service ($25 per transaction)
Mobile banking app
ATM locator service
pros: Auto loan rates advertised as low as 1.95% APR, well below market averages for established members
Free access to 30,000+ ATMs nationwide through Co-Op network with any checking account
Multiple overdraft protection options including savings transfers, money market transfers, and credit lines
No-cost mobile banking app with remote deposit capability for member convenience
Skip-A-Payment program offering temporary cash flow relief for qualified members
Tiered checking accounts with Union Pride option offering rewards and relationship benefits
Comprehensive lending menu including auto, boat, RV, motorcycle, and personal loans in one institution
cons: Membership restricted to IBEW union members and eligible family connections, excluding general public
Paper statement fees of $3.50 per cycle charged unless specific account type waives (undisclosed which accounts qualify)
Courtesy Pay overdraft service charges $25 per transaction when overdrafts occur, creating potential debt spiral during financial stress
Website lacks published rates for most loan products beyond auto loans, requiring direct member inquiry
Limited transparency on loan qualification requirements and eligibility criteria for membership categories
best_for: IBEW union members and their families seeking competitive auto financing and comprehensive financial services
Electrical workers and union-affiliated employees in the Pasadena, Texas area prioritizing union-aligned banking
Members wanting free nationwide ATM access through Co-Op network and mobile-first banking convenience
Credit union members seeking skip-a-payment programs and overdraft protection options during cash flow fluctuations
diagnosis: IBEW Local 66 Federal Credit Union is best for union-affiliated electrical workers and their families seeking competitive rates and comprehensive financial services in the Pasadena area. The primary caveat is membership eligibility restricted to IBEW members—general consumers cannot join—and fee structures ($3.50 paper statements, $25 Courtesy Pay charges) that warrant careful account management.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: I.B.E.W. Lu 66 — NCUA-insured credit union in PASADENA, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
